outcome_text: The organization's personnel and partners receive AI risk management training to enable them to perform their duties and responsibilities consistent with related policies, procedures, and agreements.
scoring_text: Skill in designing and delivering AI risk management training programs for organizational personnel and external partners, including needs assessment, curriculum development, training delivery, competency evaluation, and alignment with organizational policies and contractual agreements.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing and delivering AI risk management training programs for organizational personnel and external partners, including needs assessment, curriculum development, training delivery, competency evaluation, and alignment with organizational policies and contractual agreements.
reference_document_element_description: Design, develop, and deliver data and AI training programs for defined organizational audiences, including needs assessment, curriculum development, delivery execution, and effectiveness measurement.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-2.2 (GOVERN) equal WIDAI OP domain. OP-T-004 is the strongest match (STS: 0.756, cosine: 0.715). Secondary: OP-S-002 (OP, cosine: 0.683), AG-S-005 (AG, cosine: 0.660).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.6/10 (raw 0.756). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessment, curriculum, delivery, development, needs, organizational, programs, training]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [agreements, alignment, competency, contractual, delivering, designing] vs WIDAI terms [audiences, data, defined, deliver, design, develop] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.926 (P=0.918, R=0.933). Balanced P/R — the 33-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: notable entailment (98.6%). "Skill in designing and delivering AI risk management training programs for organ" shows logical implication toward "Design, develop, and deliver data and AI training programs for defined organizat" — stronger than typical for cross-framework competency pairs, reinforcing the STS-based relationship.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.715 ≈ STS 0.756 (gap 0.041). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, assessment, curriculum, delivery, development] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 32.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessment, curriculum, delivery, development, needs, organizational, programs, training]. NIST AI RMF-only: [agreements, alignment, competency, contractual, delivering, designing]. WIDAI-only: [audiences, data, defined, deliver, design, develop].
